comments.body: LGTM
Changes LGTM, but some tests aren't hip

/lgtm
/hold
Feel free to hold cancel when tests pass.
Large quantities of test failures - does this need a rebase?
@mheon let's try that :D
/approve
@haircommander Any chance we can get a rebase for the 1.6.0 release?

@mheon sure, should we do 2.0.1 (the eventual new conmon)? 

I'll need @lsm5 to integrate conmon into fedora before this could merge
If we can get 2.0.1 images, that'd be good - otherwise, ensuring 2.0.0 is probably sufficient.

> @mheon sure, should we do 2.0.1 (the eventual new conmon)?
> 
> I'll need @lsm5 to integrate conmon into fedora before this could merge

done on rawhide already. I'll get it on the rest asap.
Conmon is now in place on F30 and f31.

Do we need new images for the CI to make it pass?

`Error: could not get runtime: please update to v2.0.1 or later: outdated conmon version`

Parsing the logs, I found a minor nit: @haircommander, can we change the string to `outdated conmon version: please update to v2.0.1 or later`? Currently, we first print `please update to v2.0.1 or later` which confused me while reading as it didn't know what to update.

@lsm5 did you use conmon v2.0.1 for 1.6-rc{1|2}? 
@vrothberg fixed the nit, and yeah we'd need v2.0.1 in the images for this to pass

> @lsm5 did you use conmon v2.0.1 for 1.6-rc{1|2}?

I'm on it right now..
@haircommander @lsm5 This should be fixed now, correct?  Does this PR Need a rebase?
@lsm5 has 1.6 made it into the PPAs? Ubuntu image build is failing cc: @cevich

On Mon, Oct 07, 2019 at 06:43:04AM -0700, Peter Hunt wrote:
> @lsm5 has 1.6 made it into the PPAs? Ubuntu image build is failing cc: @cevich 

errr .. I had disabled the autobuild job for some reason (I guess to avoid
sending -rc to ppa).. I'll trigger it now ...
> 
> 
> -- 
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly or view it on GitHub:

-- 
Lokesh
IRC, GitHub: lsm5
GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
https://keybase.io/lsm5
@haircommander if you need to build VM images, might want to rebase onto or peek at #4189
@haircommander are we using new images here to pick up a new conmon?  (np if so, just checking)
I just did the old tool chain, do we not need to now conmon is updated via dnf?
Nope you did the right thing, I was just curious if that was the reason and if current images don't contain a new enough conmon.  (I thought I rolled up new ones last week for this)

@cevich good to know.

btw re: the in_podman problem. that'll have to be force merged through, right? then the post install will update the in_podman image and all will be well?
@haircommander Can we merge this now?  or does it need a rebase to merge?
this is ready
/lgtm

Hung on a failed test
Restarted podman-in-podman but failures might be legitimate
sorry folks, I think I missed the step of updating the dockerfile. ~I think in_podman should pass now?~

/lgtm
/hold
hold cancel when tests pass.

@cevich am I correct in saying that this PR needs to be merged before the changes in the dockerfile get used in the `in_podman` task? If that were the case, this would need to be force merged when all other tests pass

manually pushed to the in_podman image. It should pass, then once we merge the post merge action will overwrite it organically. can I have a new lgtm @rhatdan @baude @vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat @mheon
happy tests PTAL
LGTM
/lgtm
/hold cancel
Note that the code will slightly change as soon as I need to merge it into https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4352
